MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar May-21-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Cynthia Chan of FOLEY & MANSFIELD (510-590-9500), appearing for defendant / moving party Cleaver -Brooks, Inc.
Geoff T. Sloniker of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party Mario Barbera

RULING:
DEFENDANT CLEAVER-BROOKS, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Cleaver-Brooks, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its initial burden under either prong of Aguilar. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that he was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Plaintiff's deposition testimony and responses to defendant's special interrogatories preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of negating an essential element of plaintiff's claim. Mr. Tornetta's declaration is insufficient to meet this burden, as when read in conjunction with plaintiff's deposition testimony, it merely creates a triable issue of material fact. The Court will use its discretion to consider the evidence submitted with defendant's supplemental papers, namely plaintiff's responses to defendant's written discovery, as plaintiff had notice and the opportunity to respond. (Weiss v. Chevron, U.S.A., Inc. (1988) 204 Cal.App.3d 1094.) Plaintiff's objections thereto are overruled. Plaintiff's objections to Mr. Tornetta's declaration are overruled. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Tara A. Lohman of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #13428 (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/TLJ)

Kathryn Lucy Hoff of WILSON ELSER MOSKOWITZ EDELMAN & DICKER LLP (415-433-0990), appearing for defendant / moving party Collins Electrical Company, Inc.
Geoff T. Sloniker of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party Mario Barbera

RULING:
DEFENDANT COLLINS ELECTRICAL COMPANY, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Collins Electrical Company, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant's moving papers properly identify the grounds upon which it bases its motion. Defendant failed to sustain its initial burden of demonstrating that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that he was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Plaintiff's deposition testimony and responses to defendant's special interrogatories preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Assuming defendant properly made an issue of substantial factor causation, defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that any exposure to asbestos attributable to defendant was a substantial factor in causing or contributing to his asbestos-related illness. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Tara A. Lohman of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #13428 (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/TLJ)
